ops.title: Round 4 follow-up execution plan and coordination brief
ops.body:
# Round 4 — follow-up handoff plan

Handoff for the five follow-up tasks filed and approved on the round-3 sign-off board (2026-07-05), plus the environment state a fresh session needs to execute them without re-deriving round 3.

## Context

Round 3 closed with all five board cards approved and five follow-ups filed as backlog cc-notes tasks, each with a plan in its description. One additional feedback item (codex refusing untrusted cwds) already shipped as spawnllm 0.5.5 — nothing to do there. This plan sequences the remaining five, carries the pointers, and defines the gates.

**The tasks (durable records; run `cc-notes task show <id>` in each repo):**

| id | repo | P | what |
|---|---|---|---|
| `9285107` | captain-hook | P2 | UserPromptSubmit signal scan sees only the user prompt — assistant walls of text never trip the gate |
| `2e78f06` | captain-hook | P3 | PostToolUse scan source: rubric case f08 passes the gate on paper, produces no verdict |
| `101e5a8` | cc-present | P2 | install-binary hook produced no binary on yasyf-home |
| `916b03d` | cc-notes | P2 | golangci-lint failures on main (lint CI job red, pre-existing) |
| `a76d792` | cc-notes | P3 | CHANGELOG history 0.6–0.14 unstamped (drifted from tags) |

**Environment state (as of 2026-07-05):** capt-hook 8.3.0 + spawnllm 0.5.5 on PyPI; cc-notes v0.16.0 everywhere (new refspec semantics: plain fetch/pull *stages* into `refs/cc-notes-sync/<remote>/*`; `sync`/`reconcile` folds); show plugin 0.1.1 on both machines; hook-tests CI green in cc-context (381) and cc-notes (238); cc-notes lint job red (that's task 916b03d). Round-3 evidence: the close-out report at https://claude.ai/code/artifact/1e4f32ea-4a73-4259-973e-57176e9334ff; the rubric panel under `/tmp/show-rubric-panel/` may not survive reboots — f08's shape is documented inside task `2e78f06`.

## Approach

**Phase A — the captain-hook pair (`2e78f06` then `9285107`), one change-set, one release.** Both are signal-scan-source issues; diagnose before designing.

1. `2e78f06` first, as diagnosis: instrument what `transcript_texts` actually feeds the Signals scorer on PostToolUse (per-message vs joined text, window semantics), reproduce f08's shape in the harness (assistant writeup ~509 chars, one numbered-list match + one "let me know what you think" match, `tool_name: Read`), find why the score misses threshold 3. The answer likely informs step 2's design.
2. `9285107`: include a bounded window of recent assistant-turn text in the UserPromptSubmit scan source. Pin the canonical failure ("option dump as prose → user replies 'hmm'") with a harness case. Then audit for newly-firing hooks: every existing pack's thresholds were tuned against the old scan source — run cc-context (381), cc-notes (238), and show (208) suites before and after.
3. Release capt-hook next minor via its tag-triggered PyPI pipeline. **Coordination point:** main carries another session's unreleased work (signals prose-scoring, deferral gate, steering pack 0.5.0) — it will ship along. Its CI is green and the `[Unreleased]` changelog documents it; headline the changelog honestly with both, and check tags **and** PyPI for the true latest before picking a version.

**Phase B — cc-present remote install hook (`101e5a8`).** Reproduce first over SSH: run `plugin/hooks/session-start.sh` / `plugin/scripts/install-binary.sh` verbatim on yasyf-home with stderr captured. Suspects in rough order: PATH in the hook's environment, GitHub release-asset naming vs the remote's `uname` output, curl/gh availability, the version-comparison guard (fixed for v-prefixes in round 2 — check it didn't over-skip). Fix in `plugin/scripts/`, bump the plugin patch version, verify with a fresh remote headless session: `which cc-present` resolves. The remote's Claude 401s over non-interactive SSH (`apiKeySource: none`, Keychain) — expected, unrelated, user-fixable only; binary install must not depend on model auth.

**Phase C — the cc-notes pair (independent of A and B).**

1. `916b03d` lint: run golangci-lint v9 with the repo's CI config locally, triage every finding — fix real issues, tune config only for genuine noise, never contort code to satisfy the checker. Gate: the lint job green on a real push.
2. `a76d792` changelog: reconstruct one dated Keep-a-Changelog section per released tag v0.6.0–v0.14.1 from `git log vX..vY`, verifying each entry's placement with `git merge-base --is-ancestor`. Docs-only; the 0.15.0/0.16.0 sections are already stamped — match their style.

## Potential Pitfalls

- **captain-hook and cc-notes are jj-colocated with live parallel sessions.** Never edit primary working copies: isolated `jj workspace add`, base on `main@origin` after `jj git fetch`, atomic commits, fetch again before push. Releases race — a parallel session has cut releases mid-task twice; verify tags + registry before picking a version.
- **The UPS scan change is ecosystem-wide.** Any pack with UserPromptSubmit hooks can newly fire. The three green suites (381/238/208) are the regression net — run them all, and treat a newly-firing hook as a finding to disposition, not noise.
- **Releasing capt-hook ships the parallel session's main.** Legitimate (it's on main, CI green) but coordinate if that session looks active; headline the changelog with their work too.
- **cc-notes task stores get reconciled/pruned by hooks; the harness session-task list vanished wholesale once in round 3.** Before relying on a filed task, re-verify it exists (`git ls-remote origin 'refs/cc-notes/tasks/*'`). The durable copies of these five were verified on their origins on 2026-07-05.
- **yasyf-home's login shell is fish** — remote one-liners need fish syntax (`set -x PATH …`, `(cmd)` substitution, `; and` / `; or`).
- **Background/workflow agents hang forever on permission prompts.** Keep ask-triggering commands (deletes under `~/.claude`, etc.) out of agent prompts; write to fresh temp dirs instead. A pending `tool_use` with no `tool_result` in an agent transcript is the tell.
- **`cc-notes sync` alone does not autoInstall** — only task-mutating commands and `init` trigger the refspec heal. Don't infer a repo is healed from a sync; check `git config --get-all remote.origin.fetch`.
- **`cc-notes init` has side effects beyond refspecs** (wires SubagentStop into `.claude/settings.json`, registers the plugin). Use it deliberately, commit what it changes, or use a task-mutating command when only autoInstall is wanted.

## Workflow Plan

Main agent (fable) orchestrates: sequences phases, dispositions review findings, writes all user-facing prose (changelog sections included — never down-route writing), cuts releases, reports.

| Phase | Shape | Agents | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| A. captain-hook pair | pipeline: diagnose → implement → review → release | opus xhigh (diagnose 2e78f06, then implement 9285107) → codex refuter via sonnet-low wrapper → fable synthesis → main agent releases | harness tests pin both cases; 381/238/208 suites green pre and post; PyPI release live; `uvx --refresh` end-to-end |
| B. cc-present hook | single | opus xhigh | fresh yasyf-home session leaves executable `cc-present` on PATH |
| C1. lint | single | opus xhigh → codex diff review if config changed | lint job green on a push to main |
| C2. changelog | main agent (fable prose) | slop-cop check + ancestry verification | every tag ≥ 0.6.0 has a dated section whose entries are ancestors of that tag |

A, B, and C run in parallel; A's two tasks are strictly sequential (diagnosis informs design).

## Verification

- Per-phase gates in the table.
- Close-out: all five cc-notes tasks marked done with evidence comments (`cc-notes task done <id>` after criteria pass), synced to their origins; CI green in every touched repo; capt-hook release live on PyPI and resolving via `uvx --refresh`; cc-present plugin version bumped and both machines updated.
ops.when: when starting execution of the five approved follow-ups from the 2026-07-05 round-3 sign-off
